note: Module 6 — cases split by whether they are real manufacturing gravity or signals that are not yet actionable. Each: what happened, what it shows, what it does NOT show, quality tag, source.

manufacturing_relevant.title: Penang OSAT / advanced-packaging cluster
manufacturing_relevant.what_happened: Decades-deep back-end cluster (Intel, Infineon, AMD, Micron, ASE, AT&S) across Penang/Kulim; ~13% of global packaging/assembly/test activity.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_shows: A mature, multi-firm back-end ecosystem a customer could rely on today — the strongest of the five and the most realistic partnership context.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_does_not_show: Not physical front-end capacity; '~13%' is activity/market share, not capacity. Does not establish partner economics.

manufacturing_relevant.title: Infineon Kulim SiC + Thailand back-end fabs
manufacturing_relevant.what_happened: Kulim: world's largest 200mm SiC power fab — Phase 1 opened Aug 2024 (~€2B), Phase 2 up to €5B. Thailand: new back-end fab in Samut Prakan (first building ready early 2026); older Bangkok/Nonthaburi back-end site divested to Malaysian Pacific Industries (completed Feb 2026) with a long-term supply agreement.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_shows: SEA's real specialty gravity is back-end and power/IDM manufacturing.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_does_not_show: Not an advanced-packaging foundry (Thailand is a back-end component base) and not front-end foundry siting.

manufacturing_relevant.title: Amkor Bac Ninh advanced-packaging campus
manufacturing_relevant.what_happened: Large advanced-packaging assembly/test campus in northern Vietnam.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_shows: Vietnam is a credible, FDI-driven back-end location; a potential partner-ecosystem reference.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_does_not_show: No front-end supply chain; does not establish customer overlap for a specialty foundry.

manufacturing_relevant.title: GF Singapore + VIS–NXP mature-node fabs
manufacturing_relevant.what_happened: GlobalFoundries US$4B expansion (opened Sep 2023; ~1.5M wpm total) and the VSMC (VIS–NXP) US$7.8B 300mm 130–40nm JV (construction from 2H 2024, first output 2027, ~55k wpm by 2029, TSMC-licensed) concentrate front-end additions in Singapore.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_shows: The region's front-end contest — and the credible non-China mature-node answer — is in Singapore, not the five; and Singapore mature-node capacity is actively expanding.
manufacturing_relevant.what_it_does_not_show: Does not indicate any front-end opportunity in the five countries.

signals_not_actionable.title: ARM × Danantara talent-training agreement
signals_not_actionable.what_happened: Framework agreement signed in London on 23 Feb 2026 (witnessed by President Prabowo and Arm CEO Rene Haas) to train up to ~15,000 Indonesians in Arm's ecosystem; first cohort ~1,000 from May 2026. ANTARA reports an initial US$150M Indonesian-government allocation to build a national semiconductor ecosystem, and six national chip-design / IP areas Indonesia would hold (e.g. automotive, IoT, data center, home appliances).
signals_not_actionable.what_it_shows: A design-talent and ecosystem-positioning signal; Indonesia's intent to move up the value chain.
signals_not_actionable.what_it_does_not_show: NOT a manufacturing commitment (Arm's commitment is design talent and architecture, not fabrication). The US$150M is an Indonesian ecosystem allocation, not a payment to Arm. 'Arm subsidy / national-curriculum lock-in / RISC-V defense' remain analyst hypotheses, not the agreement's text.

signals_not_actionable.title: National semiconductor talent targets
signals_not_actionable.what_happened: Malaysia 60,000 by 2030; Vietnam ~50,000 by 2030; Thailand ~80,000 (5-year plan); Philippines ~128,000 upskilling.
signals_not_actionable.what_it_shows: Direction and political will; useful for trend reading.
signals_not_actionable.what_it_does_not_show: Targets are not deployable-engineer stock and not capacity; must not be mixed with actual graduate/employment numbers.

signals_not_actionable.title: Indonesia silica-downstreaming roadmap
signals_not_actionable.what_happened: Policy roadmap (2025–2035) to build domestic silicon/wafer capability.
signals_not_actionable.what_it_does_not_show: No electronic-grade processing or manufacturing base exists yet; a roadmap, not capacity.
